task_identity.title: Reconcile Git tracked files, filesystem files, generated outputs, symlinks, binaries, and ignored release inputs

preconditions: HEAD equals 9cf56e149a105026b072c9073d7e87b93103966e or an approved updated audit cut exists.
Every in-scope tracked/generated file is present in FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv and assigned to a named reviewer.
The current behavior, public claim tier, assumptions, affected consumers, and rollback are recorded before editing.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line, including tests, examples, generated output, workflow, documentation, and relevant Git history; record blob and SHA-256 identities.
Write a current-behavior table covering inputs, validation, state, transitions, outputs, errors, resources, time, identity, persistence, concurrency, and public claims.
Enumerate all plausible semantic interpretations and construct at least one counterexample or misuse for each rejected interpretation.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable IDs and exact accepted/rejected cases; obtain lead approval before implementation.
Implement the smallest coherent change and delete, quarantine, or version any contradictory legacy/default/fallback path.
Add positive, malformed, empty, min/max, non-finite, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future, capacity, cancellation, restart, and migration tests as applicable.
Add property/metamorphic, differential/oracle, fuzz, mutation-sensitive, concurrency, resource, and integration tests appropriate to the claim tier.
Generate evidence from a clean checkout with exact tools, commands, configs, seeds, data/models, platform/hardware, raw outputs, exit status, and checksums.
Update schemas, API snapshots, migration, examples, README/docs, claim/evidence ledger, support matrix, and exact downstream pins.
Have an independent reviewer reproduce the decisive evidence and review every changed line plus relevant unchanged context.
Run the subsystem gate, complete wave gate, full locked CI, and every affected downstream conformance job.
Close only after all twenty lenses are resolved with evidence; otherwise remove/narrow the claim and leave the task OPEN.
mandatory_counterfactuals: correct version string with wrong contract/algorithm digest
clean synthetic result under nonrepresentative distribution
timeout or capacity exhaustion followed by convenience fallback
feature unification activating a privileged/experimental path
partial migration accepted through default values
crash between decision/output and durable evidence
simple but odd input unlikely to resemble training examples
required_evidence: positive and negative vectors or fixtures
coverage, fuzz/mutation or model results where applicable
handoff_task_contract.completion_rule: No prose-only closure. Every acceptance condition and evidence item must pass on the exact candidate; otherwise mark NOT_CLAIMED or NO_GO.

twenty_lenses.name: Claims and scope
twenty_lenses.question: Can a reader infer more than the exact evidence tier supports? Separate implemented, verified, validated, deployment-qualified, field-validated, and not-claimed.

twenty_lenses.name: First-principles semantics
twenty_lenses.question: Are inputs, state, transitions, outputs, invariants, units, and failure states explicit rather than implied by names?

twenty_lenses.name: Mathematics and statistics
twenty_lenses.question: Are definitions, assumptions, estimands, finite-sample behavior, multiplicity, uncertainty, calibration, and failure regions correct and explicit?

twenty_lenses.name: Type and state integrity
twenty_lenses.question: Can invalid combinations be constructed through safe APIs, deserialization, FFI, feature flags, defaults, or migration?

twenty_lenses.name: Time, ordering, and replay
twenty_lenses.question: Are clock domains, freshness, deadlines, sessions, epochs, generations, sequence numbers, duplicates, restart, and rollover unambiguous?

twenty_lenses.name: Identity and provenance
twenty_lenses.question: Can every result, command, model, dataset, scene, schema, configuration, build, and evidence artifact be traced immutably?

twenty_lenses.name: Authentication and cryptography
twenty_lenses.question: Are canonical bytes, domain separation, algorithms, identities, rotation, revocation, downgrade resistance, and failure semantics complete?

twenty_lenses.name: Authority and safety
twenty_lenses.question: Who may observe, advise, intend, authorize, publish, apply, and confirm? Can any fallback or optional component widen authority?

twenty_lenses.name: Hostile inputs and parsers
twenty_lenses.question: What happens with duplicates, unknown fields, unsafe integers, NaN/Inf, Unicode ambiguity, malformed nesting, path tricks, and contradictory metadata?

twenty_lenses.name: Resource and denial-of-service bounds
twenty_lenses.question: Are bytes, allocations, dimensions, states, queues, retries, threads, log volume, GPU/CPU work, and serialization bounded before expensive work?

twenty_lenses.name: Concurrency and lifecycle
twenty_lenses.question: Are initialization, callback ownership, races, cancellation, selected events, cleanup, crash consistency, and shutdown fully specified?

twenty_lenses.name: Determinism and reproducibility
twenty_lenses.question: Can independent clean machines reproduce code generation, builds, fixtures, simulations, proofs, benchmarks, evidence, and archives?

twenty_lenses.name: API, FFI, and SemVer
twenty_lenses.question: Is the stable surface minimal and are panic, ownership, allocation, thread safety, features, MSRV/runtime, and compatibility explicit?

twenty_lenses.name: Schema, wire, and language parity
twenty_lenses.question: Is there one normative semantic source and do all languages/encodings accept and reject the same corpus?

twenty_lenses.name: Configuration and deployment
twenty_lenses.question: Can invalid profiles start? Are paths, secrets, permissions, ACLs, certificates, environments, startup order, and rollback safe?

twenty_lenses.name: Observability and forensics
twenty_lenses.question: Can disabled, unavailable, stale, incompatible, insufficient, anomalous, denied, and internal-fault states be distinguished without leaking secrets?

twenty_lenses.name: Verification and evidence quality
twenty_lenses.question: Does each claim have the right proof: theorem, model, property, fuzz, mutation, clean-room, real-router, statistical, or physical evidence?

twenty_lenses.name: Ecosystem composition
twenty_lenses.question: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Prisoma, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, ROS, and external authority relationships explicit and acyclic?

twenty_lenses.name: Human factors and governance
twenty_lenses.question: Are naming, docs, examples, runbooks, review roles, incident response, support, deprecation, and withdrawal usable under stress?

twenty_lenses.name: Counterfactual and quirky-case review
twenty_lenses.question: How would a malicious peer, rushed operator, odd-but-simple dataset, future maintainer, or AI-generated patch falsify the intended claim?

affected_surface_inventory.rationale: The exact ignore boundary is consumed by Git-backed inventory and the registered verifier; executable signed-D semantics classify .gitignore as a build-or-deployment public surface.
Canonical retained inventory consumed by the product verifier, registered independent verifier, and co-added product tests.
Create-once bounded inventory generator and exact-snapshot verifier whose identity and semantics are frozen by the registered verifier.
Exact 98-test positive, negative, lifecycle, parser, concurrency, and resource suite frozen and executed by qualification evidence.

normative_controls.statement: The verifier SHALL require one adjacent merge-free F to I to C to D transition with the exact frozen F path set, a merge-free first-parent path from I to the exact current HEAD, and no alternate current-commit substitution.

The F tree SHALL contain exactly 353 regular blobs and I SHALL modify only .gitignore and add only the ledger, product tool, and product tests, forming exactly 356 regular blobs with the exact four-path implementation diff.
The retained ledger SHALL be canonical UTF-8 CSV with the exact 72-column schema, 356 unique byte-sorted paths, and path equality with the I tree.
Every source row SHALL bind the exact F commit, F tree, Git mode, object ID, SHA-256, byte count, line count, and content kind recomputed from Git objects.

Every non-self row SHALL bind the exact clean I snapshot reconstructed solely from the F and I Git trees, later protocol additions SHALL NOT enter that inventory, and contradictory source, index, or current identities SHALL be rejected.

The ledger self row SHALL use the frozen self-exclusion sentinel and SHALL NOT claim a self-contained content digest or staged identity; the actual I ledger SHALL instead be externally bound as a regular 100644 Git blob with independently recomputed SHA-256.
The capture SHALL have no untracked, ignored, filesystem-only, or otherwise unclassified extra path beyond the exact I tree and self sentinel.

Every retained path SHALL have independently reproduced language, content-aware format, text/binary/control classification, category, and generated-candidate classification, and a reviewed row SHALL reject every suffix/content contradiction marker.

Every initial generated classification SHALL remain UNKNOWN except where an exact frozen rule proves YES; every rule-proven value and generator SHALL remain unchanged, an initial UNKNOWN SHALL resolve to YES or NO only through an evidence-backed completed review with confirmed provenance, and every YES row SHALL name a generator present in the inventory or the single frozen external Cargo generator token.

The product helper SHALL expose exact-I snapshot retention verification, while the registered verifier SHALL independently reconstruct the inventory without importing, executing, or treating that helper as its sole oracle.
All source, index, untracked, ignored, filesystem, and inventory digests SHALL be independently recomputed with the frozen domain-separated canonical encoding.

The ledger and every single Git blob read by the registered verifier SHALL be bounded to at most 4,194,304 bytes, and first-parent history SHALL be bounded to at most 1,024 commits in agreement with the central registry envelope; Git output, tree bytes, CSV cells, rows, paths, execution time, and verifier output SHALL also remain bounded, and any bound or command failure SHALL fail closed without fallback.
The verifier SHALL emit only the exact twelve-field runtime result bound to F, I, C, D, current commit, frozen program hashes, and selected claim outcome.

Every later current commit SHALL retain the exact I .gitignore, tool, tests, committed ledger mode, and all ledger immutable fields; only the twenty frozen review columns in the exact current-commit blob may change, an accepted review may retain recorded defects, and unstaged worktree bytes SHALL NOT satisfy a review gate.
All inventoried entries SHALL be exact regular Git blobs with modes 100644 or 100755, and content verification SHALL use bounded Git-object reads rather than following worktree links.

Every qualification evidence body, activation evidence JSON body, retained raw activation artifact, and review body SHALL use its exact typed versioned schema or artifact contract, canonical keys and bytes where applicable, exact lifecycle identities, chronological commands and results, executable technique bindings, exact reviewed evidence file and stream identities, complete semantic scope, and cross-record agreement. The wave gate SHALL bind separately hashed stdout and stderr, exactly thirty ordered PASS markers, zero FAIL markers, then one unique exact count line immediately followed by the unique complete final success epilogue; no other line may contain either gate-owned trailer marker. The hosted-CI attempt log ZIP SHALL be a separately captured Git blob cross-bound to A03 by exact capture metadata, file identity, compressed size, expanded size, and archive structure. Empty, placeholder, unknown-key, contradictory, failed, substituted, over-bound, or independence-violating evidence SHALL be rejected.

The I commit SHALL retain tools/release/current-file-review-ledger.py with SHA-256 56b8af777db52e6c1e721bcdae92636f84c4ded8a1b3bdfbc85689d2835910d7 and tools/release/test_current_file_review_ledger.py with SHA-256 c2a96b114d518f06e0f8387a0921551e1522c8a948c3b077e4f444c981084d32; the registered verifier SHALL require the exact 98 frozen product tests and any other bytes or test set SHALL be rejected before qualification.

Ledger generation SHALL require an absent canonical target and use one atomic no-replace creation; an existing target or concurrent winner SHALL remain untouched, no replacement option or fallback SHALL exist, post-inventory failure SHALL NOT unlink the canonical target, and a restart SHALL verify but never regenerate an existing output.

I SHALL retain the exact .gitignore bytes with SHA-256 cfcafb758561ee79536d05b834cd618e995982df699c8f8f860088e60acfb9f9, including exactly one non-negated .env, .mypy_cache/, and .ruff_cache/ pattern; .gitignore SHALL be recorded as BUILD_OR_DEPLOYMENT with PUBLIC_CLAIM_REVIEW_REQUIRED and the sole affected public surface.

Every row in the initial I ledger SHALL be UNASSIGNED, UNREVIEWED for file, provenance, and license review, UNKNOWN for all public/security/science/authority classifications, and empty in every review evidence, decision, defect, test, disposition, and completion field; later named assignment belongs to the successor review task.

mandatory_counterfactuals.statement: correct version string with wrong contract/algorithm digest
clean synthetic result under nonrepresentative distribution
timeout or capacity exhaustion followed by convenience fallback
feature unification activating a privileged/experimental path
partial migration accepted through default values
crash between decision/output and durable evidence
simple but odd input unlikely to resemble training examples

combined_attack_matrix.statement: valid signature plus stale epoch or wrong route
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: Exact commit routing, source identity, and typed lifecycle records bind this interaction.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: authenticated peer plus oversized payload and expensive logging
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: Git streams, ledger cells, files, logs, time, and memory are bounded before acceptance.
Canonical CSV syntax is insufficient without independently reconstructed semantic equality.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: green unit tests plus divergent generated binding
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: Generated status, generator linkage, immutable retention, and exact program identities are jointly checked.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: nominal statistic plus missing lifecycle/projection evidence
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: T001 makes no nominal-statistic, lifecycle-projection, or population inference claim.
combined_attack_matrix.falsifier: A T001 outcome begins asserting a statistic, population estimate, or lifecycle projection.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: ALLOW then crash before/after publication and restart
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: T001 exposes no ALLOW decision or authority-publication operation; create-once file interruption is covered separately by N18.
combined_attack_matrix.falsifier: A T001 interface begins emitting ALLOW decisions or publishing authority-bearing decisions.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: matching package version plus wrong algorithm/contract digest
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: Version labels cannot substitute for the exact product and domain-separated digest identities.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: timeout or optional-component loss plus convenience fallback
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: Capacity, deadline, and unavailable no-replace primitives fail closed without convenience fallback.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: correct local ACL file plus wrong real router
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: T001 has no router, remote ACL decision, or network authority surface.
combined_attack_matrix.falsifier: A T001 implementation begins relying on a real router or remote ACL for inventory acceptance.

combined_attack_matrix.rationale: The exact full repository distribution is reconstructed; a clean subset cannot satisfy path equality.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: canonical payload plus noncanonical signing bytes
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: Canonical typed bodies, detached signatures, and domain-separated bytes are bound independently.
Only exact regular entries and the frozen create-once surface are accepted; privileged extras fail.
The exact schema and initial review state reject partial/defaulted migration while retaining later-field boundaries.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: clock rollback plus apparently valid TTL/lease
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: T001 records UTC chronology but accepts no TTL, lease, or clock-derived runtime authority.
combined_attack_matrix.falsifier: A T001 interface begins accepting a TTL, lease, or freshness decision from wall-clock time.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: cancellation request plus already-selected event and duplicate effect
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: Create-once cancellation boundaries preserve one winner, typed uncertainty, and no duplicate regeneration.

handoff_command_mapping.rationale: The signed updated audit cut and exact active-claims record supersede the stale source hash.
The replacement performs bounded Git-object and physical-view reconciliation with create-once output.
CH-T002 owns named reviewer assignment and review-packet construction after inventory bootstrap.
CH-T003 owns the repository claim-language scan and its generated report.

threat_model.asset_or_claim: secret-ignore boundary and clean inventory input
threat_model.preconditions: write access to .gitignore or local secret files
threat_model.sequence: remove or negate .env, introduce local caches, then attempt inventory or staging
threat_model.trust_boundary: tracked ignore configuration versus untracked local workspace
threat_model.observable_symptoms: exact .gitignore hash mismatch or rejected ignored extras
threat_model.worst_consequence: secret material enters history or a machine-local path is presented as complete inventory
threat_model.recovery: stop qualification, restore exact ignore bytes, rotate any exposed secret, and regenerate only from a clean isolated checkout
threat_model.residual_risk: force-adding an ignored secret remains possible outside this task and requires repository access controls
threat_model.claim_impact: qualification fails; no secret-protection guarantee beyond the exact tracked ignore boundary is claimed

threat_model.preconditions: write access to the ledger parent namespace
threat_model.sequence: create or swap the target at the no-replace boundary while generation is active
threat_model.trust_boundary: pinned parent directory versus canonical target namespace
threat_model.observable_symptoms: existing-target refusal, no-replace conflict, or typed unknown creation result
threat_model.worst_consequence: generation cannot prove which entry is canonical and fails closed without clobbering either candidate
threat_model.recovery: inspect the canonical target and reserved temporary, then verify the surviving target without automatic regeneration
threat_model.residual_risk: a same-identity local writer can force a denial of service or require manual disposition
threat_model.claim_impact: at-most-one accepted creation is claimed; automatic replacement or recovery authority is not claimed

threat_model.preconditions: ability to type-swap a path after lstat
threat_model.sequence: replace a regular entry with a FIFO before the open call
threat_model.trust_boundary: path metadata check versus opened descriptor
threat_model.observable_symptoms: nonblocking open and post-open regular-type rejection
threat_model.worst_consequence: without the control the inventory process could block indefinitely
threat_model.recovery: fail the inventory, restore a regular Git-backed entry, and rerun in a clean checkout
threat_model.residual_risk: a hostile local writer can still cause bounded failure
threat_model.claim_impact: bounded fail-closed behavior is retained; availability under a hostile writer is not claimed

threat_model.actor: malformed-command-output injector or correlated parser defect
threat_model.preconditions: substitution of ls-tree output or incorrect size grammar
threat_model.sequence: supply a canonically padded gitlink size marker that a naive token-count parser rejects or misclassifies
threat_model.trust_boundary: Git byte stream versus typed source-entry parser
threat_model.observable_symptoms: exact padded-grammar acceptance for gitlinks and rejection of noncanonical records
threat_model.worst_consequence: source inventory diverges from Git while appearing structurally valid
threat_model.recovery: reject the capture and rerun with the exact Git executable and frozen parser bytes
threat_model.residual_risk: the qualified repository contains regular blobs only, so gitlink support remains diagnostic outside I
threat_model.claim_impact: exact I remains regular-only; no gitlink qualification claim is added

threat_model.preconditions: ability to stage or edit bytes that differ from signed commits
threat_model.sequence: substitute index or current-worktree content for an exact F or I object
threat_model.trust_boundary: signed commit objects versus mutable index and worktree
threat_model.observable_symptoms: source, index, current, or retained immutable identity mismatch
threat_model.worst_consequence: bytes absent from the signed lifecycle are falsely certified
threat_model.recovery: discard mutable substitutions and reconstruct solely from exact commit objects
threat_model.residual_risk: Git object compromise is outside the repository-local verifier claim
threat_model.claim_impact: qualification fails on any object substitution

threat_model.actor: oversized repository input or accidental resource exhaustion
threat_model.preconditions: input reaches a frozen byte, row, path, stream, or time limit
threat_model.sequence: exhaust a bound and request cached, partial, or best-effort fallback
threat_model.trust_boundary: bounded verifier acceptance versus convenience behavior
threat_model.observable_symptoms: explicit capacity or deadline error and no success output
threat_model.worst_consequence: an incomplete ledger is treated as complete
threat_model.recovery: reduce the candidate within frozen bounds or amend the preimplementation contract; never reuse partial output
threat_model.residual_risk: successful operation at every declared hard ceiling is not claimed
threat_model.claim_impact: only fail-closed validation bounds and exact-candidate measurements are claimed

threat_model.actor: process interruption or repeated operator invocation
threat_model.preconditions: a prior attempt created a target or retained reserved temporary
threat_model.sequence: restart generation and attempt to recreate or overwrite instead of inspect and verify
threat_model.trust_boundary: prior creation state versus new process intent
threat_model.observable_symptoms: existing target or reserved temporary is reported without mutation
threat_model.worst_consequence: the only durable candidate is overwritten or provenance is lost
threat_model.recovery: retain all entries, inspect typed state, and run exact verification against the surviving target
threat_model.residual_risk: manual disposition of preserved temporaries is required
threat_model.claim_impact: automatic restart regeneration and replacement are explicitly not claimed

threat_model.preconditions: ability to edit mutable review columns before successor review
threat_model.sequence: populate reviewer, decision, evidence, or completion fields in the initial I ledger
threat_model.trust_boundary: inventory bootstrap versus later named review authority
threat_model.worst_consequence: inventory creation is misrepresented as completed assignment or review
threat_model.recovery: restore all initial review columns and defer assignment to CH-T002
threat_model.residual_risk: later review quality remains outside T001 and requires successor evidence
threat_model.claim_impact: T001 claims inventory completeness only, not assignment or review completion

threat_model.preconditions: generator and verifier share the same faulty parser or digest framing
threat_model.sequence: produce matching but incorrect ledger and product-test results
threat_model.trust_boundary: product implementation versus registered independent verifier
threat_model.observable_symptoms: independent Git-object reconstruction or differential digest mismatch
threat_model.worst_consequence: correlated false acceptance of an incomplete inventory
threat_model.recovery: reject qualification and repair both implementations against the external Git oracle
threat_model.residual_risk: bounded representative differential cases are not an exhaustive proof of Git itself
threat_model.claim_impact: the product helper is never the sole qualification oracle

threat_model.actor: later reviewer or automated ledger editor
threat_model.preconditions: write access to the retained ledger after I
threat_model.sequence: change immutable identity, use an adverse disposition, or erase a recorded defect while presenting review completion
threat_model.trust_boundary: frozen inventory fields versus permitted review columns
threat_model.observable_symptoms: immutable mismatch or non-accepting/incomplete review rejection
threat_model.worst_consequence: different bytes or adverse findings are presented as accepted review
threat_model.recovery: restore frozen immutable values and record a complete accepting or not-applicable disposition without concealing defects
threat_model.residual_risk: substantive review correctness is a successor-task responsibility
threat_model.claim_impact: only the exact mutable-field boundary is qualified

misuse_resistant_interfaces.justification: This changed build-or-deployment surface controls whether local secrets and caches are ignored.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.correct_example: Retain the exact .env, .mypy_cache/, and .ruff_cache/ patterns and inventory a clean isolated checkout.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.wrong_example: Remove or negate .env and treat a local-secret-bearing worktree as the signed inventory input.
non_proofs: an ignore pattern does not prevent force-add or prove secret rotation
misuse_resistant_interfaces.evidence_tier: exact tracked-byte and clean-checkout repository verification

misuse_resistant_interfaces.justification: The operator-facing command creates the canonical ledger exactly once and must not imply replacement authority.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.correct_example: Generate into the absent canonical target in a clean isolated checkout, then retain the reported identity.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.wrong_example: Run generation over an existing target or add a replacement fallback after a conflict.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.exact_refusal_or_error: ledger already exists; create-once generation refuses <path>
non_proofs: successful creation is not assignment, file review, release approval, or physical-media durability

misuse_resistant_interfaces.justification: Verification can be misused against current mutable bytes instead of the exact I snapshot.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.correct_example: Verify with the exact source and implementation commit identities and reject immutable drift.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.wrong_example: Treat a clean current worktree or matching schema version as proof of the exact I inventory.
non_proofs: a valid CSV header or clean worktree is not exact snapshot identity

misuse_resistant_interfaces.justification: Later edits are permitted only in the frozen review columns and can otherwise overstate review completion.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.correct_example: Leave I unassigned, then later record a named evidence-backed accepted review while retaining disclosed defects.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.wrong_example: Preassign I, alter immutable identity, hide a defect, or mark an adverse disposition as reviewed success.
non_proofs: an assigned reviewer is not a completed review
recorded defects do not automatically invalidate an accepted disposition

limitations: .gitignore is a BUILD_OR_DEPLOYMENT affected public surface under executable signed-D protocol semantics; this classification does not add or qualify a public correctness, deployment, or release claim.
CH-T001 establishes inventory completeness, not completed file assignment or review; every I row intentionally starts UNASSIGNED and successor CH-T002 owns named assignment.
Create-once generation provides no authority to replace, update, or automatically recover an existing canonical target; preserved uncertain state requires inspection and exact verification.
No release, deployment, publication, tag, DOI, Zenodo, archive, or field authority is granted.
Successful-operation performance at hard resource ceilings is not claimed; retained distributions cover the exact 356-row candidate and hard ceilings are fail-closed validation bounds.

The CH-T001 wave-gate decision is based on the pinned gate's exit status, exact ordered PASS and FAIL markers, unique gate-owned summary and epilogue chronology, and complete final success epilogue; benign child-tool diagnostics, including successful test summaries containing '0 failed' and nonempty cargo stderr, are retained and digest-bound but are not treated as independent gate failures.
The hosted-CI attempt log ZIP is retained as a separate raw Git blob capped at 2097152 compressed bytes and 4194304 expanded bytes; exceeding either bound fails closed and requires a new freeze.
